synopsis.lastAssistant: The live doctrine host now serves the new rules at version `1.0.150`, and I verified the remote files directly. I’m committing the doctrine repo before deploying Storyboard.
The commit succeeded locally as `66080c9`. Push is blocked by DNS resolution to GitHub from this sandbox, so the work is committed but not on GitHub and not deployed.

Deployment is blocked by S3 connectivity, so the evidence artifact will be local fallback evidence unless upload starts working later. I’m recording the local build now against the exact committed files and the cache-busted entrypoints.

The source catalog is correct now: only `demos` remains among those four IDs. The public `sites.mullmania.com/catalog.json` endpoint is still serving an older cached copy, so I’m going to refresh the site index/cache before checking the UI-facing feed again.
